predicted_questions.question_text: Thanks, and congrats on the quarter. Relative to the assumptions you used in January, how did close‑in pricing and the booking curve for the Mediterranean and West‑Coast Mexico itineraries actually reconcile — did you need to take pricing actions or promotions to re‑fill inventory, and roughly what percentage of your Q2/Q3 capacity was booked at close‑in prices below your January APD assumptions? In short, did you leave meaningful yield on the table that you expect to recapture later?
predicted_questions.rationale: Conor repeatedly presses on booking dynamics and close‑in pricing (recurring_concerns.core_topics[0]); management flagged a short‑term moderation in Med and West‑Coast Mexico bookings, an updated lower Net Yield outlook versus January, and said bookings have recently rebounded — this question asks for the numeric close‑in reconciliation versus January assumptions.

predicted_questions.question_text: Quick follow on capital allocation: you returned $1.1bn this quarter and repurchased $836m with $1bn remaining under the program — given $6.9bn liquidity and leverage below 3x after the bond deal, should we expect a more deliberate pace of opportunistic buybacks this year (i.e., use most or all of the remaining $1bn), or is the board still prioritizing cash/flexibility for Perfecta, new builds and other investments?
predicted_questions.rationale: Conor frequently revisits buybacks and board capital‑allocation philosophy (recurring_concerns.core_topics[1]); management disclosed repurchases, remaining authorization, liquidity and leverage metrics in the presentation, so he will press on cadence and the tradeoff versus strategic investments.

predicted_questions.question_text: One modeling question on costs: you guide Q2 Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel up ~4.6–5.1% and said first‑half cost growth is higher than second half driven by dry docks and travel disruptions — can you break that Q2 delta out roughly between incremental dry‑dock expense, crew/air travel disruption costs, and secular inflation? And separately, are the Perfecta/digital and AI investments creating any structural cost pressure, or are they expected to be margin‑accretive over the medium term?
predicted_questions.rationale: Conor presses for cost cadence and investment‑related cost detail (recurring_concerns.core_topics[2]); the CFO called out sizable Q2 ex‑fuel cost increases, dry‑dock days and crew travel impacts, and referenced Perfecta — this asks for a quant split to model H2 cadence and the ongoing cost impact of strategic investments.
